Engineering Intelligent Systems from Data to Production
I am a Machine Learning and GenAI engineer with experience across applied research, distributed data processing, forecasting, natural language processing and full-stack product engineering.

My work focuses on connecting rigorous data preparation, leakage-safe experimentation, model evaluation and software engineering to build AI systems that are reliable, explainable and useful in real applications.
I hold a Master of Science in Computer Science from Blekinge Institute of Technology in Sweden and a Bachelor of Technology in Computer Science and Engineering from JNTU Kakinada.

Predicting Risk Level in Life Insurance Application
A machine-learning research project comparing classification approaches for predicting life-insurance applicant risk levels across original and modified datasets.
Multi-class insurance-risk prediction using applicant features and comparative model evaluation.
Life-insurance applicant dataset containing 59,629 records and 127 features.
Data preprocessing, feature modification, classifier comparison and evaluation using accuracy, precision, recall and F1-score.
Random Forest achieved 53.79% accuracy on the original dataset.
Random Forest achieved 52.80% accuracy on the modified dataset.
